codeReview.summary: The fix narrows wandering-merchant cleanup to SHOP_CLOSE events carrying the merchant's own npcId, which directly addresses the prior regression without breaking the broader SHOP_CLOSE listeners used by normal NPC state transitions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The SHOP_CLOSE contract now includes an optional `{ npcId }` payload and UIScene maintains `shopNpcId` so callers can distinguish which NPC shop actually closed, but this event-payload convention is not documented in shared library notes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/scenes/UIScene.ts:647,656,669,4502 stores/emits `shopNpcId`; src/scenes/ZoneScene.ts:2144-2150 filters despawn on `data?.npcId === 'wandering_merchant'`; .factory/library/architecture.md documents EventBus generally but not the SHOP_CLOSE payload contract.

summary: Reviewed the prior failed feature, the fix feature handoff/transcript, both commits (`db607e0` and `558fc20632a32cb197a21fe1a77966f9be99a74e`), and the relevant source/tests. The new implementation correctly preserves the wandering merchant across unrelated panel closes by tagging SHOP_CLOSE with the closed shop's npcId and only despawning on the merchant's own close event. I found no remaining blocking issues in this fix.
